danger_signals.visual_form: Bright contrasting bullets/energy beams distinct from background
Distinct mechanical/organic enemy sprites with clear silhouettes
Large-scale sprite with complex geometry, screen-filling dimensions

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear screen areas without enemies or projectiles
Distinct item sprites with bright coloration, different from enemies

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varied mechanical/biomechanical designs suggesting multiple unit types

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense enemy formations, rapid projectile fire, screen clutter increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies advance and attack in coordinated patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen dominance by single large entity with distinct visual design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss executes patterned attack sequences with variable difficulty phases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scrolling background environments change, enemy type variety increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty escalates through enemy count and attack complexity

environmental_storytelling.example: Distinct stage environments suggesting different battle locations/terrain
Progressive introduction of new enemy types and visual complexity per stage

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Predictable movement patterns, coordinated fire within formations
Complex attack patterns with multiple phases, directional fire changes
Single hitpoint loss causes visible feedback, destruction on depletion

notes: Bio Metal is a vertical shmup with minimal narrative framing. World-state communication relies entirely on visual density, enemy type variety, and difficulty progression. No faction politics or ideological markers are present. Danger is communicated purely through sprite visibility, projectile patterns, and screen saturation. The game prioritizes mechanical challenge signaling over worldbuilding.
